Author: Allison
EnhancedContent:
On February 13, 2026 we [announced changes to the required network configuration](https://github.blog/changelog/2026-02-13-network-configuration-changes-for-copilot-coding-agent/) for [Copilot coding agent](https://docs.github.com/copilot/concepts/agents/coding-agent/about-coding-agent), taking effect on February 27.

These changes only affect teams running Copilot coding agent on [self-hosted runners](https://docs.github.com/actions/concepts/runners/self-hosted-runners) or [larger runners with Azure private networking](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-cloud@latest/admin/configuring-settings/configuring-private-networking-for-hosted-compute-products/configuring-private-networking-for-github-hosted-runners-in-your-enterprise).

These changes have now taken effect. Copilot will apply [subscription-based network routing](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-cloud@latest/copilot/how-tos/administer-copilot/manage-for-enterprise/manage-access/manage-network-access), connecting to a different host depending on the Copilot plan of the user who initiated the agent task:

- Copilot Business: `api.business.githubcopilot.com`
- Copilot Enterprise: `api.enterprise.githubcopilot.com`
- Copilot Pro and Pro+: `api.individual.githubcopilot.com`

If your network is still configured to allow connections to `api.githubcopilot.com` for Copilot coding agent, you can now drop this host from your allowlist.
Title: Network configuration changes for Copilot coding agent now in effect
Description:
On February 13, 2026 we announced changes to the required network configuration for Copilot coding agent, taking effect on February 27. These changes only affect teams running Copilot coding agent…

The post [Network configuration changes for Copilot coding agent now in effect](https://github.blog/changelog/2026-03-02-network-configuration-changes-for-copilot-coding-agent-now-in-effect) appeared first on [The GitHub Blog](https://github.blog).
